steps.title: Contrast specimen types
steps.content: The first reference organism is a holotype, the exact specimen used for the original description. The next reference organism is a neotype, which acts as a reference but is not the originally described specimen.

steps.title: Signal the category difference
steps.content: Choice B signals addition, not opposition. Choice C signals reluctant concession. Choice D would make the neotype an example of a holotype, which it is not. The remaining choice marks the contrast between the two specimen categories. The answer is A.
